title: Varel DeVay

{{Quote|Um, I'm not sure that I'm suited to accompany the exploration team. I'm a scientist and not very useful in dangerous situations.|Varel DeVay to Kaiya Adrimetrum|<ref name="PF" />}}
'''Varel DeVay''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|female]] [[Xenobiologist/Legends|xenobiologist]] from the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core]]. Growing up in a pampered environment, DeVay enjoyed the finest education, and worked for several research companies throughout [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]. Offered a new job on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Kal'Shebbol/Legends|Kal'Shebbol]], [[Sector/Legends|sector]] [[Capital/Legends|capital]] of the [[Kathol sector/Legends|Kathol sector]], she was forced into working on biological weapons for the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Warlord/Legends|warlord]] [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] [[Kentor Sarne]]. Rescued from her service to him when the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] [[Battle of Kal'Shebbol|liberated]] the planet in [[8 ABY/Legends|8 ABY]], DeVay signed on as a member of the crew of the [[CR90 corvette/Legends|CR90 corvette]] ''[[FarStar]]'', which had been charged with [[DarkStryder Campaign|tracking down]] the rogue Moff after he fled from Kal'Shebbol during the battle. DeVay was assigned to duties in the [[Starship/Legends|ship's]] [[Hydroponics/Legends|hydroponics]] bay, and was later a member of the landing party to the planet [[Binaros]]. The party inadvertently brought back the spores of a [[feeder plant]] from the surface, and as it grew, the plant caused malfunctions within the ship's systems. DeVay was attacked by the plant's mobile [[Feeder plant#Feeders|feeders]], and was instrumental in developing a [[Poison/Legends|countermeasure]] that neutralized the threat.

==Biography==
===Early life===
Varel DeVay grew up on a small [[Planet/Legends|planet]] in the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core]], and led a privileged life. She attended the best [[University/Legends|universities]], and received an excellent education which opened up several career choices for her. DeVay was fascinated by the myriad [[Alien/Legends|alien]] [[Species/Legends|species]] in [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]], and this interest led her to choose [[xenobiology]] as her [[Xenobiologist/Legends|intended career]]. Graduating from her studies in [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]], DeVay worked for several research corporations, switching jobs often to climb the corporate ladder and gain a measure of financial security. In the space of five [[Standard year/Legends|years]], she had worked in four jobs on three separate worlds.<ref name="PF" />

In [[8 ABY/Legends|8 ABY]], DeVay was offered a position with a small corporation on the planet [[Kal'Shebbol/Legends|Kal'Shebbol]], the [[Sector/Legends|sector]] [[Capital/Legends|capital]] of the [[Kathol sector/Legends|Kathol sector]] in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]], at twice her current salary. Inside a [[Standard month/Legends|month]], DeVay had left her existing position and reported for work. Upon arrival, she found that the company did not exist. [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] [[Kentor Sarne]], the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Warlord/Legends|warlord]] who ruled the planet, had plotted to lure [[Scientist/Legends|scientists]] to his domain by creating false companies that promised employment to workers;<ref name="PF" /> once on Kal'Shebbol, they were subject to the planet's strict immigration controls imposed by Sarne and were forbidden to leave.<ref name="TDC">''[[The DarkStryder Campaign]]''</ref> DeVay had fallen for Sarne's ruse, and was essentially drafted into [[Moff Sarne's forces|Sarne's forces]], where she was forced to work on biological weapons against her will.<ref name="PF" />

===Service with the ''FarStar''===
DeVay was freed from her forced servitude when the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] [[Battle of Kal'Shebbol|liberated]] the planet in 8 ABY. She contacted her family, and [[Varel DeVay's father|her father]] begged her to return home to them. DeVay was too proud, though, and could not admit that her father was right about her situation, so she refused. Having learned of the New Republic [[CR90 corvette/Legends|CR90 corvette]] ''[[FarStar]]''{{'s}} [[DarkStryder Campaign|mission]], she signed on as a member of the crew; the [[New Republic Defense Force/Legends|New Republic military]] could not staff the [[Starship/Legends|vessel]] due to manpower shortages, so they recruited from the population of Kal'Shebbol. The ship was going deep into the Kathol sector to track down Moff Sarne, who had fled the planet with his forces when the New Republic had attacked. DeVay was assigned to oversee the [[Hydroponics/Legends|hydroponics]] bay of the ship and was placed in charge of the vessel's three [[J9 worker drone/Legends|J9 worker drones]]. DeVay hated [[Droid/Legends|droids]] and constantly reviewed their work, making sure it was up to her own exacting standards. She constantly put in requests to the command crew for more biological beings to assist her.<ref name="PF" />

Several weeks after launching from Kal'Shebbol, the ''FarStar'' crossed the [[Marcol Void/Legends|Marcol Void]], an expanse of [[Realspace/Legends|space]] absent of any planetary [[Star system/Legends|systems]]. After a long journey, the ship came across the planet [[Binaros]]. Initial [[Sensor/Legends|sensor]] scans and analysis from the [[Laboratory/Legends|laboratory]] staff convinced [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Kaiya Adrimetrum]] that the planet offered a good source of fresh foodstuffs and [[Water/Legends|water]]. She ordered a reconnaissance party to the surface to take a closer look and ordered DeVay to accompany them. DeVay protested at her inclusion in the team, claiming that she would be of little use in any dangerous situations they may encounter. Adrimetrum countered this by telling her that she could identify potential food on the ground rather than waste time and resources [[Shuttle/Legends|shuttling]] samples back to the ship only to discover they were useless. She also advised DeVay to check out a [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]] from the armory just in case.<ref name="PF" />

Shuttled down to the surface of Binaros aboard [[Brophar Tofarain]]'s ship, the ''[[Muvon]]'', DeVay was ordered by the mission leader, [[Executive officer/Legends|First Officer]] [[Gorak Khzam]], to collect and analyze vegetation and [[Plant/Legends|plants]] for edible food. DeVay set up a portable science station in the landing party's main camp area, and left other members of the party to collect samples. They brought their findings back to DeVay, and she analyzed them with her equipment to determine their suitability. During the expedition, the [[Defel/Legends|Defel]] [[Scout/Legends|scout]] [[Kl'aal]] accidentally picked up the spores of the native [[feeder plant]], and unknowingly took them back to the ''FarStar'' when the landing party returned to the ship. The spores detached themselves from Kl'aal and settled in the isolated areas of the [[Corvette/Legends|corvette]]. Although most of the spores [[Death/Legends|died]] off, some settled, finding the ship both warm and rich in the metallic nutrients they needed. Taking root in the forward lower hold, the spores began their life-cycle, spawning into the [[Feeder plant#Mother plant|mother plant]]. In turn, the mother plant produced its [[Feeder plant#Feeders|feeders]] to go forth and gather the required nutrients, food and water it needed.<ref name="PF" />

[[File:Vareldevay02.jpg|thumb|right|Varel DeVay holds off feeders in the ''FarStar''{{'s}} hydroponics bay.]]
The actions of the feeders caused puzzling malfunctions throughout the ship, and they also siphoned seventy-five liters of water from the hydroponics lab's tank that fed the irrigation system. DeVay noticed the theft, and reported it to the command staff. To her dismay, Khzam did not take the theft seriously, but DeVay knew that she needed all the water available to keep the hydroponics bay operating at maximum efficiency. In the hold, the mother plant had entered a stage of its life cycle where it required [[Nitrogen/Legends|nitrogen]] from the [[Blood/Legends|blood]] of living animals, and it directed its feeders to obtain the required element. Shortly after reporting the theft, DeVay was attacked by the feeders in the hydroponics bay. She managed to contact the command staff and inform them that she was under attack. Cornered by the creatures, she kept them at bay with a [[vibrospade]] until Khzam—who had ordered all available personnel to respond to the [[Distress signal/Legends|distress call]]—arrived. The crew members drove the feeders off, and followed them back to where the mother plant had taken root. DeVay observed the mother plant, and realized that she could concoct a gaseous [[Poison/Legends|poison]] that would kill the plant. The poison took several [[Standard hour/Legends|hours]] to create, but it neutralized the threat that the plant presented.<ref name="PF" />

DeVay continued to serve on the ''FarStar'' during its voyage through the Kathol sector, where her knowledge of xenobiology proved useful to the mission on several occasions. She attempted to improve the corvette's methods for food preservation, and worked hard analyzing plant samples. During one experiment, DeVay fell prey to the hallucinations that were a common side effect of traveling though the [[Kathol Rift/Legends|Kathol Rift]]—a volatile area of dense [[Nebula/Legends|gas clouds]] and [[lightstorm]]s—and believed that the sample she was working on had sprouted [[Limb/Legends|tentacles]] and was crushing the breath out of her. After a moment, the hallucination faded, and DeVay found herself unharmed in the lab with the plant sample motionless on the sensor equipment.<ref name="TKR">''[[The Kathol Rift]]''</ref> After several months, the ''FarStar'' tracked Sarne to the [[Kathol system/Legends|Kathol system]], and [[Battle of Kathol System|ended the threat]] he posed to the region.<ref name="E">''[[Endgame]]''</ref>

==Personality and traits==
Varel DeVay stood 1.8 [[Meter/Legends|meters]] tall, and both her [[Eye/Legends|eyes]] and [[Hair/Legends|hair]] were [[Color/Legends|brown]]. Ambitious and driven, DeVay was intent on climbing the corporate ladder and achieving financial security, an obsession that led her to change jobs frequently; it also led her to temporarily lose her freedom. Although proud and stubborn, DeVay was considered meek by some who knew her, and she readily admitted that she had no desire to be involved in potentially dangerous situations. However, when the need arose and her safety was threatened by feeder plants on the ''FarStar'', DeVay was able to rise above her fear and fight back to save her life.<ref name="PF" />

A xenobiologist, DeVay had extensive knowledge of alien species and languages, and was able to determine the value of natural resources. She had expertise surviving in forests, and could ride animals. She could operate sensor equipment, program and repair [[Computer/Legends|computers]], and dispense [[Medic/Legends|first aid]]. A perfectionist, she insisted that the work of the droids assigned to the hydroponics bay alongside her measured up to her demanding standards. DeVay hated droids and disliked having to work alongside them on the ''FarStar''. She constantly requested more biological assistance in her department, but none was available. To assist her in her work, she generally carried [[Datapad/Legends|datapads]] containing relevant information on plants and animals, a [[Comlink/Legends|comlink]], a [[Glowrod/Legends|glowrod]], a [[recording rod]], a hand [[Microscope/Legends|microscope]] and a hand [[vibro-hoe]].<ref name="PF" />

==Behind the scenes==
Varel DeVay was created for the [[West End Games]] [[Roleplaying game|roleplaying-game]] adventure ''[[Plant Food]]'', written by [[Chris Doyle]], which was featured in ''[[The Kathol Outback]]'', which was a supplement for ''[[The DarkStryder Campaign]]''. She was one of the main characters in the adventure, but did not appear in any of the subsequent adventures. A brief mention of the character was made in the crew notes in ''[[The Kathol Rift]]'', the second supplement for ''The DarkStryder Campaign'', where it was noted that the Gamemaster and the players had the freedom to develop the character as they saw fit.
